description.en: A small, rustic Swiss roll with a crackling, deeply baked crust around a light, irregular crumb, traditionally baked touching in pairs, then torn apart. The classic partner for a grilled sausage, and an everyday roll across German-speaking Switzerland.
description.de: Ein kleines, rustikales Schweizer Brötchen mit knisternder, tief ausgebackener Kruste und lockerer, unregelmäßiger Krume, traditionell paarweise gebacken und dann auseinandergerissen. Der klassische Partner zur Bratwurst, alltäglich in der Deutschschweiz.
description.es: Un panecillo suizo rústico y pequeño, de corteza crujiente y bien horneada y miga ligera e irregular, horneado tradicionalmente en pareja y separado luego a mano. El acompañante clásico de la salchicha a la parrilla, un pan cotidiano en la Suiza alemana.
description.fr: Un petit pain suisse rustique à la croûte craquante bien cuite et à la mie légère et irrégulière, traditionnellement cuit en paire puis séparé à la main. L'accompagnement classique de la saucisse grillée, un pain du quotidien en Suisse alémanique.

origin.note: St. Gallen is often cited as the Bürli's home; near-identical rolls (Bauernbrötli, Ruch-Bürli) appear region-wide under local names.

ingredients.note: 1 g in the pre-dough, 6.7 g in the main dough

yield.shape: oval rolls baked in touching pairs (Doppelbürli)

keeping.note: The crackling crust softens fast; best the day it's baked, or split and toasted the next day.

method.title: Build the pre-dough
method.summary: Mix spelt wholegrain flour, water and a little yeast into a soft paste. Ferment 2 hours at room temperature, then retard overnight in the fridge.

method.title: Mix the main dough
method.summary: Combine the pre-dough with white and higher-extraction wheat flours, rye flour, water, salt, a little more yeast, and a spoon of mature wheat sourdough starter. Knead in stages, working in the last of the water and then olive oil gradually, until the dough passes the windowpane test.

method.title: Bulk ferment with folds
method.summary: Let the dough rise about 2.5 hours at warm room temperature, giving it four gentle stretch-and-folds early on to build strength while keeping the crumb open and irregular.

method.title: Divide gently
method.summary: Portion the risen dough into small pieces, handling it as lightly as possible so the bubbly structure survives.

method.title: Shape and pair
method.summary: Round each piece loosely and set them down in touching pairs, seam-side down.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Let the paired rolls rise, covered, for about 30 minutes.

method.title: Bake with steam
method.summary: Slide onto a very hot baking stone, steam heavily at first, then ease off both the steam and the oven temperature until the crust is dark and crackling.

source.author: Lutz Geißler
source.adaptation: Baker's percentages reconciled by resolving the stiff starter (TA 150) into its flour/water components; method paraphrased into fewer steps.
